message:
fix(migrate,doctor,tui,tools): harden migration flow with pre-schema backup, improve doctor diagnostics for absolute fallback paths

Migration robustness (lib.rs, legacy.rs):
- Create backup BEFORE schema migration so a corrupted schema step can
  restore the pristine copy and continue with timestamp-only migration.
- After successful migration, call enforce_runtime_fts_cleanup() and
  switch journal_mode to WAL for consistency.
- Replace PRAGMA_SETTINGS_SQL with PRAGMA_DB_INIT_BASE_SQL in both the
  main and legacy migration paths.
- Resolve database path via sqlite_absolute_candidate_path() before
  opening, so `handle_migrate_cmd` works when the configured relative
  path is missing but the absolute path exists.
- Require --force for Unknown timestamp format before any mutating
  operation (moved check earlier in the flow).
- New is_sqlite_recovery_error_message() guard: on schema migration
  failure that looks like SQLite engine instability, restore backup
  and continue with timestamp-only migration instead of aborting.

Doctor diagnostics (lib.rs):
- New DoctorOpenContext struct tracks configured_path, opened_path,
  used_absolute_fallback, and fallback_due_to_missing_configured_path.
- Doctor checks now emit "warn" (not "ok") when the database is only
  accessible via absolute fallback because the configured path is
  missing, giving operators clear signal that config needs updating.
- sqlite_doctor_sanity_with_health_probe returns 3-tuple with the
  missing-configured-path flag; db_file_sanity check propagates it.
- sqlite_absolute_fallback_path / sqlite_absolute_candidate_path now
  use .is_file() instead of .exists() to avoid matching directories.
- New sqlite_path_is_explicit_relative() helper replaces ad-hoc
  starts_with("./") / starts_with("../") checks.
- Handle sqlite_path() errors in db_file_sanity check instead of
  silently skipping.
- New test: doctor_check_reports_db_file_sanity_when_database_url_is_invalid.
- Updated existing absolute-fallback test assertions for warn status.

TUI search scroll fix (search.rs):
- Detail pane scroll extent calculation now correctly handles the
  JsonTree view mode by computing wrapped lines from the live JSON
  tree state rather than the cached rendered detail text, which was
  stale for tree mode and caused incorrect scroll bounds.
- Added #[allow(clippy::items_after_test_module)] to suppress lint.

Outbox SQL cleanup (resources.rs):
- Removed unnecessary "m." table alias prefixes from outbox SELECT
  queries since there is no JOIN; the alias was a leftover from an
  earlier multi-table query.

E2E test coverage (test_migration.sh):
- New Case 0: exercises `am migrate --force` directly on the legacy
  Python-format fixture, verifying exit code, integrity_check, column
  type conversions (projects.created_at, messages.created_ts,
  file_reservations.created_ts), and data preservation (subject text).

Beads issue tracker updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"br-3jz52","title":"T1.5: Add animated chart transitions for smooth data updates","description":"Charts should not jump when new data arrives. Instead, data points should smoothly transition
to their new positions. This creates a professional, polished feel.

APPROACH:
- Maintain previous and current data point positions
- On each render frame, interpolate between previous and current positions
- Use ease-out timing function for natural deceleration
- Transition duration: 200ms (12 frames at 60fps)

This applies to all chart widgets (LineChart, BarChart, Canvas heatmap).

Implementation in ChartDataProvider: add `interpolated_points(t: f64)` method that returns
data points lerped between previous and current state.

FILES: tui_widgets.rs (ChartDataProvider), tui_screens/dashboard.rs, tool_metrics.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Data point transitions animate over 200ms
- [ ] Ease-out timing function applied
- [ ] No visual glitches during transitions
- [ ] Can be disabled via env var for CI/testing

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:25:07.177790757Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:25:07.177720555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["animation","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3jz52","depends_on_id":"br-333hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3jz52","depends_on_id":"br-3q8v0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3jz52","depends_on_id":"br-rk4gw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":737,"issue_id":"br-3jz52","author":"LilacLake","text":"Implemented chart-transition scaffolding and wired it into active chart surfaces.

Changes:
- Added reusable `ChartTransition` helper in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_widgets.rs` with cubic ease-out interpolation, disable-motion bypass, and reset support.
- Added new unit tests for transition interpolation/disable-motion/reset behavior in the same file.
- Dashboard now animates throughput series via `ChartTransition` and honors `AM_TUI_CHART_ANIMATIONS` + reduced-motion flags (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/dashboard.rs`).
- Tool Metrics latency bar chart now renders from animated cached rows driven by `ChartTransition` (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs`).

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --check` passes after formatting.
- Workspace `cargo check --all-targets` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` are currently blocked by pre-existing compile failures in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/cache.rs` (IndexMap import/trait-bound issues unrelated to this change set).
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:05:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3k16e","title":"R1.4: Implement TOON serializers for Agent Mail domain types (messages, agents, reservations as tabular arrays)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:48.439159076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:47:21.113670953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:47:21.113648330Z","close_reason":"15 TOON-friendly domain types in robot.rs with Serialize derives + toon round-trip tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3k16e","depends_on_id":"br-3i7fz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":738,"issue_id":"br-3k16e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R1.4: TOON Serializers for Domain Types

## What
Implement `Serialize` (serde) for all domain types that robot commands return, ensuring TOON-friendly structure. The key insight: TOON's tabular array format (`items[N]{col1,col2}: ...`) works best when arrays contain uniform structs with flat fields.

## Where
- Core model types in `mcp-agent-mail-core/src/models.rs` (if not already Serialize)
- New response types in `mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/` for robot-specific views

## Domain Types Needed
Each robot command returns a specific data struct. These must be flat and tabular-friendly:

1. **StatusData** — inbox_summary, recent_activity, anomalies[], my_reservations[], top_threads[]
2. **InboxEntry** — id, priority, from, subject, thread, age, ack_status, importance
3. **TimelineEvent** — seq, timestamp, kind, summary, source
4. **OverviewProject** — slug, unread, urgent, ack_overdue, reservations
5. **ThreadMessage** — position, from, to, age, importance, ack, subject, body
6. **SearchResult** — id, relevance, from, subject, thread, snippet, age
7. **MessageContext** — full message + thread position + adjacent summaries
8. **ReservationEntry** — agent, path, exclusive, remaining, granted_at
9. **MetricEntry** — name, calls, errors, error_pct, avg_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms
10. **HealthProbe** — name, status, latency_ms, detail
11. **AnomalyCard** — severity, confidence, category, headline, rationale, remediation
12. **AgentRow** — name, program, model, last_active, msg_count, status
13. **ContactRow** — from, to, status, policy, reason, updated
14. **ProjectRow** — slug, path, agents, messages, reservations, created
15. **AttachmentRow** — type, size, sender, subject, message_id, project

## TOON Optimization Guidelines
- Keep structs FLAT (no nested objects in tabular arrays)
- Use short field names where possible (agent not agent_name)
- Timestamps as relative strings ("5m ago") for TOON, ISO-8601 for JSON
- Boolean fields as true/false (TOON renders compactly)
- Numeric fields without units (units in column header)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 15 types derive Serialize
- Unit test: serialize each type to JSON, convert to TOON, verify parseable
- TOON output for a 5-row array uses <50% of the JSON token count
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:13Z"},{"id":739,"issue_id":"br-3k16e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R1.4: TOON Serializers for Domain Types

## What
Implement `Serialize` (serde) for all domain types that robot commands return, ensuring TOON-friendly structure. The key insight: TOON's tabular array format (`items[N]{col1,col2}: ...`) works best when arrays contain uniform structs with flat fields.

## Where

## Domain Types Needed
Each robot command returns a specific data struct. These must be flat and tabular-friendly:

1. **StatusData** — inbox_summary, recent_activity, anomalies[], my_reservations[], top_threads[]
2. **InboxEntry** — id, priority, from, subject, thread, age, ack_status, importance
3. **TimelineEvent** — seq, timestamp, kind, summary, source
4. **OverviewProject** — slug, unread, urgent, ack_overdue, reservations
5. **ThreadMessage** — position, from, to, age, importance, ack, subject, body
6. **SearchResult** — id, relevance, from, subject, thread, snippet, age
7. **MessageContext** — full message + thread position + adjacent summaries
8. **ReservationEntry** — agent, path, exclusive, remaining, granted_at
9. **MetricEntry** — name, calls, errors, error_pct, avg_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms
10. **HealthProbe** — name, status, latency_ms, detail
11. **AnomalyCard** — severity, confidence, category, headline, rationale, remediation
12. **AgentRow** — name, program, model, last_active, msg_count, status
13. **ContactRow** — from, to, status, policy, reason, updated
14. **ProjectRow** — slug, path, agents, messages, reservations, created
15. **AttachmentRow** — type, size, sender, subject, message_id, project

## TOON Optimization Guidelines
- Keep structs FLAT (no nested objects in tabular arrays)

## Acceptance Criteria

- TOON output for a 5-row array uses <50% of the JSON token count

-{"id":"br-3kwox","title":"Agent Mail runtime: SQLite malformed image blocks inbox/ack/send","description":"Problem: Agent Mail CLI operations (mail send, list-acks, ensure_project) fail with 'Query error: database disk image is malformed' in /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust. Impact: multi-agent coordination is blocked. Repro: run cargo run -q -p mcp-agent-mail-cli -- mail send against this project and observe malformed image error. Needed: root-cause analysis, DB repair path, and regression tests for concurrent send/ack/list-acks.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:

MCP tool calls (health_check/ensure_project/macro_start_session) also timeout (-32004).","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:07:26Z"},{"id":742,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"LavenderGoose","text":"Progress 2026-02-19: reproduced failure when DATABASE_URL is set to sqlite:///data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/storage.sqlite3. Root cause: sqlite URL parsing interprets this as relative path data/projects/... (not absolute), and that relative file in-repo is malformed; absolute /data/... file is healthy. Implemented fix in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs: DbPool::new now calls resolve_sqlite_path_with_absolute_fallback(), which switches to healthy absolute sibling only when configured relative path is malformed and absolute counterpart is healthy; explicit ./ and ../ paths remain untouched. Added regression tests resolve_sqlite_path_prefers_healthy_absolute_when_relative_is_malformed and resolve_sqlite_path_keeps_explicit_dot_relative_paths. rch validation: cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --lib passed remotely; rch cargo test encountered worker linker env issue (missing -lsqlite3).","created_at":"2026-02-19T21:16:29Z"},{"id":743,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"OrangePlateau","text":"Revalidated rch gate behavior while working br-25kea. Current broad db test failures remain dominated by worker environment FD exhaustion (Too many open files / os error 24), including schema/pool/query tests opening temp DBs. This appears infra-host limit related, not deterministic code-path regressions in the new concurrent-writer changes. See latest rch run: env RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db --all-targets -> 695 passed / 37 failed, failures all rooted in EMFILE.","created_at":"2026-02-20T04:15:26Z"},{"id":744,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"MagentaRiver","text":"MagentaRiver runtime gate note (2026-02-20): after latest db pool/cache changes, focused query-layer validations are green via rch (check/clippy + queries::tests 65/65), but full crate sweep on worker still fails with OS error 24 (Too many open files). Repro command: rch exec -- env RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db --all-targets. Result: 696 passed / 37 failed; failures are dominated by EMFILE while opening temp sqlite files / Tantivy index files (pool, queries, schema, search_v3 tests). This looks like remaining remote FD-exhaustion behavior rather than deterministic logic regression in the concurrent-writer query changes.","created_at":"2026-02-20T05:05:56Z"},{"id":745,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 GoldSeal progress: added regression coverage for malformed relative DATABASE_URL fallback in tests/e2e/test_db_corruption.sh. Changes: (1) try_session now accepts either raw path or full sqlite URL, (2) new helper tool_call_succeeded() to distinguish non-error tool responses, (3) new Case 8 validates sqlite:///relative malformed path succeeds and relative malformed file remains untouched (proving fallback behavior). Validation: bash tests/e2e/test_db_corruption.sh passed (10 pass / 0 fail). Artifacts: tests/artifacts/db_corruption/20260220_202907/.","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:29:55Z"},{"id":746,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 GoldSeal follow-up: strengthened new fallback regression case to exercise full flow (ensure_project + register_agent + send_message) under malformed relative sqlite URL () while verifying the relative malformed file remains untouched. Switched check 1/1b to non-mutating open + sidecar-aware health probe (sqlite_file_is_healthy) with malformed-relative absolute fallback handling; added regression test doctor_check_uses_absolute_fallback_for_malformed_relative_database_url. rch validation: cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli doctor_check_uses_absolute_fallback_for_malformed_relative_database_url -- --nocapture => pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:00:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-3kwox.1","title":"Reproduce request_contact DB errors and isolate failing SQL path","description":"Observed while onboarding PurpleHorizon on 2026-03-02: send_message is blocked by contact policy, and request_contact / macro_contact_handshake return generic database errors.\n\nEvidence:\n- resource://tooling/diagnostics shows contact tools failing systematically (request_contact calls=36, errors=36; macro_contact_handshake calls=47, errors=33).\n- Health diagnostics report DB pool saturation warnings (utilization 100%) and elevated tool error rate.\n- Repro via MCP tool call: request_contact(project_key='/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust', from_agent='PurpleHorizon', to_agent='MaroonBridge') returns generic DB error.\n\nGoal for this bead: instrument and reproduce the exact database error cause in contact request flow, then patch to restore handshake path reliability.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurpleHorizon","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:15:46.546382041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:16:58.966652509Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3kwox.1","depends_on_id":"br-3kwox","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:15:46.546382041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1068,"issue_id":"br-3kwox.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress 2026-03-02: reproduced contact-path failure via MCP tool calls. request_contact(project_key='/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust', from_agent='PurpleHorizon', to_agent='MaroonBridge') consistently returns generic DATABASE_ERROR. macro_contact_handshake also fails similarly. In-session behavior became intermittent: send_message to self previously succeeded (message ids 1168/1169) but later also returned DATABASE_ERROR, indicating broader transient DB failure surface beyond contact policy gating. Health endpoint still reports green, so failure is likely in a specific query/transaction path not captured by high-level liveness checks.","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:16:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3l1t","title":"Fix inconsistent expiry boundary in in-memory reservation filters","description":"Commit 5bd9a46 fixed the SQL boundary from 'expires_ts < ?' to 'expires_ts <= ?' but three in-memory filters still use the old strict-less-than boundary: resources.rs:3571, resources.rs:3600, reservations.rs:642. This creates a window where a reservation at exact expiry is excluded from the active list (correct) but also excluded from the expired cleanup (incorrect), creating an orphaned state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:32:05.321774571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:40:37.498071088Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:40:37.498052132Z","close_reason":"Fixed 5 inconsistent expiry boundary comparisons across resources.rs, reservations.rs, cleanup.rs, and guard/lib.rs. All now use inclusive boundary (<=) matching the SQL fix in 5bd9a46.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-3lc7f","title":"T10.8: Execute cross-platform validation matrix for all migrated native commands (Linux/macOS/Windows)","description":"## Objective
Establish an explicit cross-platform acceptance matrix for all migrated native commands so portability claims are proven, not assumed.

## Work
- Define OS matrix coverage for Linux, macOS, and Windows environments (or documented equivalent CI images).
- Validate each migrated command path (`am ci`, `am flake-triage`, `am bench`, `am check-inbox`, serve enhancements, `am golden`, share wizard, verify-live, native E2E harness).
- Stress platform-sensitive behavior: path normalization, newline/encoding differences, process spawning, lock semantics, env-file handling, and shell quoting assumptions.
- Capture deterministic per-OS artifacts and troubleshooting bundles.
- Open follow-up beads for any platform-specific gaps discovered.

## Deliverable
A reproducible cross-platform evidence set that confirms migrated native workflows are truly portable for end users.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Matrix execution covers every migrated command surface in this epic across Linux/macOS/Windows.
- Platform-sensitive edge cases are explicitly exercised and documented (filesystem, process, network, env, and terminal behavior).
- Per-OS runs emit machine-readable evidence bundles (JSON summaries, command traces, stdout/stderr, timing, environment fingerprint).
- Any failures produce actionable follow-up beads with dependency links back to affected migration tracks.
- Final epic audit (`T10.6`) consumes this matrix as mandatory closure evidence.\n\n

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this Progress 2026-02-23 (JadeCarp):
- Claimed br-3lc7f.
- Added CI job `.github/workflows/ci.yml` -> `native-command-matrix` with required Linux/macOS/Windows runs.
- Matrix captures per-case artifacts under `tests/artifacts/cli/native_command_matrix/<os>/`:
  - `commands/<case_id>/{command.txt,stdout.txt,stderr.txt,exit_code.txt,timing_ms.txt}`
  - `environment.json`
  - `summary.json`
- Updated skip gate so `skip-guard` requires `native-command-matrix` success.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/SPEC-script-migration-matrix.md` (new T10.8 cross-platform matrix section + artifact contract)
  - `docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md` (gate matrix + evidence map rows for cross-platform portability)
- Local validation completed:
  - workflow YAML parse check: `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml")'`
  - matrix command surface checks: `am <command> --help` for all listed matrix cases.

@@ -270,6 +270,35 @@ INSERT INTO file_reservations (id, project_id, agent_id, path_pattern, exclusive
 VALUES (1, 1, 1, 'src/legacy/**', 1, 'legacy reservation', '2026-02-24 15:33:00', '2026-12-24 15:33:00', NULL);
 SQL
 
+# ===========================================================================
+# Case 0: Direct Rust migrate path handles legacy fixture without corruption
+# ===========================================================================
+e2e_case_banner "am migrate handles legacy sqlite fixture directly"
+DIRECT_DB="${WORK}/direct_migrate.sqlite3"
+cp -p "${PYTHON_DB}" "${DIRECT_DB}"
+set +e
+DIRECT_MIGRATE_OUT="$(
+    HOME="${TEST_HOME}" \
+    PATH="${PATH_BASE}" \
+    AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli \
+    DATABASE_URL="sqlite:///${DIRECT_DB}" \
+    "${AM_BIN}" migrate --force 2>&1
+)"
+DIRECT_MIGRATE_RC=$?
+DIRECT_INTEGRITY="$(sqlite3 "${DIRECT_DB}" "PRAGMA integrity_check;" 2>/dev/null)"
+DIRECT_PROJECT_TYPE="$(sqlite3 "${DIRECT_DB}" "SELECT typeof(created_at) FROM projects WHERE id=1;" 2>/dev/null)"
+DIRECT_MESSAGE_TYPE="$(sqlite3 "${DIRECT_DB}" "SELECT typeof(created_ts) FROM messages WHERE id=1;" 2>/dev/null)"
+DIRECT_RES_TYPE="$(sqlite3 "${DIRECT_DB}" "SELECT typeof(created_ts) FROM file_reservations WHERE id=1;" 2>/dev/null)"
+DIRECT_SUBJECT="$(sqlite3 "${DIRECT_DB}" "SELECT subject FROM messages WHERE id=1;" 2>/dev/null)"
+set -e
+e2e_save_artifact "case_00_direct_migrate_output.txt" "${DIRECT_MIGRATE_OUT}"
+e2e_assert_exit_code "direct am migrate exits cleanly" "0" "${DIRECT_MIGRATE_RC}"
+e2e_assert_eq "direct am migrate integrity_check is ok" "ok" "${DIRECT_INTEGRITY}"
+e2e_assert_eq "direct am migrate projects.created_at type" "integer" "${DIRECT_PROJECT_TYPE}"
+e2e_assert_eq "direct am migrate messages.created_ts type" "integer" "${DIRECT_MESSAGE_TYPE}"
+e2e_assert_eq "direct am migrate file_reservations.created_ts type" "integer" "${DIRECT_RES_TYPE}"
+e2e_assert_eq "direct am migrate preserves message subject" "Legacy migration message" "${DIRECT_SUBJECT}"
+
 # ===========================================================================
 # Case 1: Run installer takeover flow
 # ===========================================================================
